_units: median wall-clock in milliseconds from pytest-benchmark default config (warmup absorbed)
_notes: All five Metal queries exceed the M1 ratio_metal_over_cpu <= 1.05 gate by 12-55x at 10M rows. The cpu_ms timings reflect Polars 1.40.1 with default optimization; metal_ms reflects the M1 end-to-end path (DataFrameScan -> Filter -> optional Project) lowered through the Python walker, predicate-AST UDF, MSL kernels, and Arrow<->MTLBuffer bridge. Kernel-only criterion numbers (target/criterion/) show cmp_i64 ~20ms and filter_scatter ~19ms per 10M rows, so even the raw kernels are slower than CPU end-to-end; the remaining ~150-180ms in metal_ms is dispatch + buffer marshalling overhead per query. Treat as the honest M1 starting point; T28 retrospective owns the gate-widening decision.
